title: galium boreale subsp. incurvatum
intro: Galium boreale subsp. incurvatum, also known as a subspecies of the Northern Bedstraw, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Galium boreale subsp. incurvatum include its ability to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce swelling and pain, and support the body's natural detoxification processes. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support kidney and urinary health.
